sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.56 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$< X R >$be a finite presentation (the words in$R$are assumed cyclically reduced). Define the length\/ of the presentation to be the sum of the number of generators and the lengths of the relators. Let$F(n)$be the number of (isomorphism types of) groups that have a presentation of length at most~$n$. What can one say about the function$F(n)$? It can be shown that$F(n)$is not recursive (D.\,Sega...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
